packageRules.description: Group packages released in lockstep from the Effect monorepo. Renovate's own `group:monorepos` preset matches on `matchSourceUrls`, which does not work here: `monorepo.json` lists `Effect-TS/effect-smol`, while the published packages point at `Effect-TS/effect`. Matching on package names instead keeps the group working no matter where the sources move.

Group packages released in lockstep from the React monorepo. Same failure as above, from a different cause: React moved GitHub orgs, so `facebook/react` now redirects to `react/react` (same repo, id 10270250). Renovate derives `sourceUrl` from the latest published release, which reports `react/react`, and it does not follow redirects when matching — so `group:monorepos`, which still lists `facebook/react`, no longer matches. Matching on package names is immune to the move.
